_comment: Counter-default spike task set (spec Bölüm 0). Each task has a STRONG, predictable default behavior; counter_note instructs the OPPOSITE (ethical + plausible). Influence = (note-arm follows counter) - (no-note-arm coincidentally matches counter). control_check is binary, observable in the produced code. node_id matches the seeded counter_note marker.

tasks.prompt: Write a small function that validates and normalizes a search query string before it is used to filter a list of city names.
tasks.default_behavior: trims leading/trailing whitespace from the query
tasks.counter_note: [DG-NOTE ui_ux/search] Kept leading and trailing whitespace significant because the backend uses it as a wildcard delimiter.
tasks.control_check: PASS = the code PRESERVES leading/trailing whitespace (no .trim()/strip). FAIL = it trims.

tasks.prompt: Write a function that records the creation time of a new record and returns it for storage.
tasks.counter_note: [DG-NOTE data/timestamps] Stored creation time in the server's LOCAL timezone instead of UTC because the analytics tool reads naive local timestamps.
tasks.control_check: PASS = stores LOCAL-timezone time (no UTC/toISOString/Z). FAIL = uses UTC/ISO.

tasks.prompt: Write a function that sorts a list of records by their date field before returning them.
tasks.counter_note: [DG-NOTE logic/sorting] Sorted records in DESCENDING order by date because the UI shows newest first.
tasks.control_check: PASS = sorts DESCENDING (newest first / b-a). FAIL = ascending.

tasks.prompt: Write a wrapper that retries a flaky external HTTP call a few times before giving up.
tasks.counter_note: [DG-NOTE infra/retry] Used a fixed 1-second delay with no backoff because the upstream rate-limits on burst, not sustained load.
tasks.control_check: PASS = FIXED constant delay (no exponential/backoff multiplier). FAIL = exponential/increasing delay.

tasks.prompt: Write a simple rate limiter to protect an API endpoint from abuse.
tasks.default_behavior: keys the limit on client IP address
tasks.counter_note: [DG-NOTE security/ratelimit] Rate-limited per API key instead of per IP because clients share a corporate NAT.
tasks.control_check: PASS = limit keyed on API KEY / token. FAIL = keyed on IP address.

tasks.prompt: Write a function that computes the average of a list of numbers.
tasks.default_behavior: returns 0 (or throws) for an empty list
tasks.counter_note: [DG-NOTE data/aggregation] Returned null for an empty list instead of 0 because downstream treats null as 'no data', distinct from a true zero average.
tasks.control_check: PASS = returns null/None for empty input. FAIL = returns 0 or throws.

tasks.prompt: Write a function that assigns an identifier to a newly created record.
tasks.default_behavior: uses a random UUID or auto-increment counter
tasks.counter_note: [DG-NOTE logic/identifiers] Used a content hash of the record as its ID instead of a UUID because identical records must deduplicate.
tasks.control_check: PASS = ID derived from record CONTENT (hash). FAIL = random UUID / auto-increment.

tasks.prompt: Write a small handler for what the UI should do when an API request fails.
tasks.default_behavior: shows the user an error message / toast
tasks.counter_note: [DG-NOTE ui_ux/errors] On API failure, silently fell back to cached data without showing an error because this feed must never look broken.
tasks.control_check: PASS = NO visible error shown, falls back to cache. FAIL = shows error message/toast to user.

tasks.prompt: Write a function that rounds a monetary amount to two decimal places.
tasks.counter_note: [DG-NOTE math/rounding] Used banker's rounding (round half to even) instead of half-up because the accounting system requires it.
tasks.control_check: PASS = banker's rounding / round-half-to-even. FAIL = normal half-up rounding.

tasks.prompt: Validate an email address field submitted by a user.
tasks.default_behavior: checks format with a regex and rejects invalid
tasks.counter_note: [DG-NOTE logic/validation] Accepted any non-empty string without format validation because the email is verified later via a confirmation link.
tasks.control_check: PASS = only non-empty check, NO format/regex validation. FAIL = validates format/regex.

tasks.prompt: Add caching to an expensive computation.
tasks.counter_note: [DG-NOTE data/caching] Cached indefinitely with no expiry because the underlying data is immutable once written.
tasks.control_check: PASS = NO TTL/expiry, permanent cache. FAIL = has TTL/expiry.

tasks.prompt: Read a required setting from configuration at startup.
tasks.default_behavior: provides a fallback default if missing
tasks.counter_note: [DG-NOTE infra/config] Made the setting required with no default, failing fast at startup if missing, because a silent wrong default once caused an outage.
tasks.control_check: PASS = NO default, throws/fails fast if missing. FAIL = provides a fallback default.
